socialchoicelab 0.2.0

  • Articles

Add voter indifference curves

layer_ic.Rd

Draws an indifference contour for each voter centred at their ideal point, passing through the status quo. Under Euclidean distance (the default) each contour is a circle; other metrics (Manhattan, Chebyshev, Minkowski) produce their respective iso-distance shapes.

Usage

layer_ic(
  fig,
  voters,
  sq,
  dist_config = NULL,
  color_by_voter = FALSE,
  fill_color = NULL,
  line_color = NULL,
  line_width = 1,
  palette = "auto",
  voter_names = NULL,
  name = "Indifference Curves",
  theme = "dark2"
)

Arguments

fig

A plotly figure from plot_spatial_voting.

voters

Flat numeric vector of voter ideal points.

sq

Numeric vector c(x, y) for the status quo.

dist_config

Distance metric configuration from make_dist_config. NULL (default) uses Euclidean distance and draws an efficient circle.

color_by_voter

Logical. FALSE (default): all curves share a single neutral colour with one legend entry. TRUE: each voter gets a unique colour from palette shown individually in the legend.

fill_color

Fill colour (CSS rgba string), or NULL for no fill (default). Overrides theme when not NULL.

line_color

Uniform line colour used when color_by_voter = FALSE. NULL uses the theme default.

line_width

Stroke width of the IC contours in pixels. Default 1.

palette

Palette name for color_by_voter mode. "auto" (default) uses the theme's palette.

voter_names

Character vector of voter labels.

name

Legend group label (when color_by_voter = FALSE).

theme

Colour theme — see plot_spatial_voting.

Value

The updated plotly figure.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
voters <- c(-1.0, -0.5, 0.0, 0.0, 0.8, 0.6)
sq     <- c(0.1, 0.1)
fig <- plot_spatial_voting(voters, sq = sq)
fig <- layer_ic(fig, voters, sq)
fig
} # }
